title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers will be inducted into the Basketball Hall of Fame with Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett as presenters.
Rivers ranks sixth on the all-time NBA coaching wins list with 1,194 victories, including a championship in 2008.
The Hall of Fame ceremony will occur on August 15 at Symphony Hall in Springfield, featuring the Class of 2026.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum expresses it's 'weird' having former teammate Jaylen Brown now as a division rival with the 76ers.
Jaylen Brown traded from the Celtics to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round and two second-round picks.
Fans reacted emotionally to Brown's trade; a viral moment included a young fan, Giovanni Jean, reacting tearfully.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's Bill Barnwell values Patriots QB Drake Maye at six first-round picks, ranking him alongside Josh Allen and Patrick Mahomes.
Maye, 23, has appeared in a Super Bowl and earned two Pro Bowl selections, making him a valuable player.
Historical QB trades, like those of Deshaun Watson and Eli Manning, have involved multiple first-round picks.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The furnished island home is located in Gilford, N.H., on Lake Winnipesaukee, offering rental potential and lake views.
Built in 1952, the cottage-style home has 1,037 square feet, 3 bedrooms, and 1 full bath, with 2025 taxes at $8,027.
The listing price options for the home are $999,500, $889,500, and $789,500, with $889,500 identified as the price.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' stars Matt Damon as Odysseus and retells Homer's epic with a human-focused narrative.
The film features nonlinear storytelling, emphasizing Odysseus’s struggle with human frailty over divine intervention.
Spectacular effects include mythical creatures and stunning IMAX visuals, showcasing Nolan's signature practical filmmaking style.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle married his partner Devin over the All-Star break.
The couple celebrated their wedding at Arya Trattoria, a North End restaurant.
Tolle, 23, has 18 career starts with a 5-7 record and a 3.59 ERA.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore anchored the Patriots' defensive line, combining for 86 pressures last season.
The Patriots' run defense improved significantly, allowing 101.7 rushing yards per game, ranking sixth in the league.
The Patriots lost Khyiris Tonga in free agency, impacting depth, but retain strong interior linemen for 2026.

title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam, 28, died in a skydiving accident in Orange, Massachusetts, due to a wind-related parachute issue.
The incident at Jumptown Skydiving involved a sudden 'dust devil,' leading to Gaddam's fall and subsequent death at Athol Hospital.
Gaddam, a Northeastern University grad from India, recently obtained his skydiving A License and worked as a software developer.

title: Is the real summer turnaround in Boston sports coming from Legacy FC?
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC achieved its first road win, defeating the Orlando Pride 1-0 in an NWSL match.
After a shaky start with five consecutive losses, Legacy FC has improved with a five-game unbeaten run.
Legacy FC will face a major challenge on Sunday against Washington Spirit, second in the NWSL standings.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 16, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Nana's Green Tea opens its new café on Newbury Street in Boston focused on matcha and Japanese-inspired drinks and foods.
About Thyme Lebanese Street Café introduces its saj-based dishes and beverages in Cambridge's Harvard Square on August 1.
C’Yool Yemeni Café and Bakery opens July in the South End, offering roasted coffee and a menu influenced by Middle Eastern cuisine.
